seoTitle: Run-o-Waters vs Runnyford — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Run-o-Waters and Runnyford. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Run-o-Waters and Runnyford.
heading: Run-o-Waters vs Runnyford — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Run-o-Waters, New South Wales and Runnyford, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Run-o-Waters has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Runnyford (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Run-o-Waters or Runnyford safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Run-o-Waters has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Runnyford scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Run-o-Waters scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Runnyford scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Run-o-Waters or Runnyford?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Run-o-Waters has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
